metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep/diff proving command for each content AC (e.g. `grep -n 'test_sync_preserves_detached_head_local_ahead_commit' docs/concurrency.md`, `grep -n 'fcntl' README.md`, `grep -n '5 cores' docs/mutation-testing.md`, `grep -n 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard' tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_leaves_effect_matrix.py`) so the executor has evidence-producing checks beyond lint.
findings.finding: The only stated verification command is `make lint` (format/lint checks), which cannot prove the *content* of any documentation change. ACs 1–4 each assert specific prose corrections (a test reference, a lock-mechanism description, a scope qualifier, a coverage note, and docstring labels) but no grep/diff commands are provided to confirm those strings actually appear in the modified files.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four bullets) and final bullet `make lint passes`
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a "Verification" section listing:
```
grep -n 'test_sync_preserves_detached_head_local_ahead_commit' docs/concurrency.md
grep -n 'fcntl' README.md && ! grep -n 'flock' README.md
grep -n '5 cores\|selected scope' docs/mutation-testing.md
grep -n 'SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard' tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_leaves_effect_matrix.py
grep -n 'structural.*tripwire\|supplemental' docs/coverage.md
```
Each must return a match; the second additionally asserts no residual `flock` mention remains.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC4 how adding `SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard` to `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf` reconciles with the module-level docstring of `test_leaves_effect_matrix.py` that explicitly frames `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf` as behavioral completeness enforcement — either scope the module docstring update or restrict the label to the function-level only.
findings.finding: `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf`'s docstring (line 514) contains no `SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard` label, but the module-level docstring of `test_leaves_effect_matrix.py` (lines 1-25) explicitly frames `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf` as a BEHAVIORAL COMPLETENESS enforcement test ('a newly-added leaf with no behavioural coverage FAILS this test'). Adding a SUPPLEMENTAL label to the function without updating the module-level docstring creates a direct internal contradiction: the module says 'behavioral,' the function will say 'supplemental/structural.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC2 (or the 'What' section) to explicitly name README.md line 198 as a second util-linux/BusyBox framing instance that must also be updated to describe the Python `fcntl` + `mkdir` dual-window lock.
findings.finding: README.md contains a second instance of the stale util-linux/BusyBox external-flock framing at line 198 ('util-linux flock is used for write serialization when present, with a mkdir fallback otherwise') that the plan's 'Why' section and narrative do not mention. The plan cites only '~lines 146-151', anchoring implementer attention there; AC2 broadly says 'drops the util-linux/BusyBox external-flock framing' but without a probe of the rest of the file the second instance could survive the edit.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's AC2, append: 'Also update README.md line ~198 (the Install section parenthetical) which repeats the same stale util-linux framing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the Why or Scope section explaining what the 'Windows claim' in README.md is (e.g., a note that external `flock` works on Linux/Windows via WSL, or a platform-support statement) and why it should be dropped alongside the util-linux/BusyBox framing.
findings.finding: AC2 contains the parenthetical '(no Windows claim)' — a removal requirement that appears nowhere in the Why, What, or Scope sections. Neither the motivation nor the scope explains what Windows claim exists in README.md, why it is being removed, or how the implementer should handle it. This is an orphan sub-requirement with no grounding in the plan body.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why section, after the README.md bullet, add: 'The README also makes a platform compatibility claim (e.g., Windows support via WSL or a similar note) that was predicated on the external flock binary and should be removed along with it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `README.md` line 198 (Install section: 'a util-linux flock is used for write serialization…') to the Scope and AC2 edit targets, since this second instance carries the same stale external-flock framing.
findings.finding: README.md contains a second stale `util-linux flock` passage at line 198 (inside the Install section: 'a util-linux flock is used for write serialization when present, with a mkdir fallback otherwise') that is not enumerated in the plan's Scope or AC2. The plan only references lines 146-151 (System prerequisites). If AC2 is implemented only at lines 146-151, the Install section will still misrepresent the implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope, change 'README.md (~lines 146-151)' to 'README.md (~lines 146-151 and ~line 198)'. In AC2, note that BOTH the System prerequisites bullet and the Install-section parenthetical must be updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC4: Clarify whether the `SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard` label belongs in the **function-level** docstring or the **module-level** docstring for each of the three named tests, resolving the inconsistency between the existing patterns in `test_leaves_registry_coverage.py` (module-level) and `test_llm_timeouts.py` (function-level), and specify which placement to use when adding the label to `test_matrix_covers_every_leaf`.
findings.finding: AC4 references a decision rule — 'confirm each test's docstring carries a SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard label' — but the placement protocol is ambiguous and inconsistent with existing practice: test_leaves_registry_coverage.py carries the label only in its MODULE docstring (line 1), not in the named test function (test_every_leaf_has_nonstub_body_structural_guard); test_no_total_runtime_timer_mechanism carries it in the FUNCTION docstring. For test_matrix_covers_every_leaf, the plan says 'add it if missing' but never specifies whether the label should go in the module docstring or the function docstring — an executor following the inconsistent existing pattern cannot resolve this deterministically.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4 — 'confirm each test's docstring carries a SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard label'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to read: 'Each named test's **function-level** docstring must carry a `SUPPLEMENTAL structural guard` label; for `test_leaves_registry_coverage.py`, move the existing module-level label into the `test_every_leaf_has_nonstub_body_structural_guard` function docstring.' (Or alternatively, standardize on module-level and update the llm_timeouts case.) Pick one convention and name it explicitly.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
